title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
summary: AI agents often claim to have searched the web but instead fall back to stale training data when blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or IP restrictions. Real web access through tools like Bright Data's Web MCP enables reliable citations, real-time results, and reduces hallucinations compared to simulated or degraded access.
writeup: Rafael Levi of Bright Data examines a common failure mode in AI agents: claiming to have searched the web while actually returning stale training data after being blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or IP restrictions. The talk argues that genuine web access matters because degraded or simulated access produces hallucinations and unverifiable answers. The concrete claim is that real retrieval through tools like Bright Data's Web MCP supports reliable citations, real-time results, and reduced hallucination relative to fallback behavior.
